{{Short description|none}}<!-- Short description INTENTIONALLY left blank per WP:SDNONE -->
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Montserrat women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Montserrat
| Badge              = Montserrat Football Association.svg
| Badge_size         = 180px
| Nickname           = ''Emerald Boys''
| Association        = [[Montserrat Football Association]] (MFA)
| Confederation      = {{nowrap|[[CONCACAF]] (North America)}}
| Sub-confederation  = [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] (Caribbean)
| Coach              = [[Angus Eve]]
| Captain            = [[Brandon Comley]]
| Most caps          = [[Alex Dyer (footballer, born 1990)|Alex Dyer]] (36)
| Top scorer         = [[Lyle Taylor]] (13)
| Home Stadium       = [[Blakes Estate Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme     = MSR
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|MSR}}
| FIFA max           = 165
| FIFA max date      = August 2014
| FIFA min           = 206
| FIFA min date      = January 2011 – January 2012, June 2012, August – September 2012
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Montserrat}}
| Elo max            = 107
| Elo max date       = February 1950
| Elo min            = 233
| Elo min date       = September 2012
| pattern_la1        = 
| pattern_b1         = _nikeprecision6ht
| pattern_ra1        = 
| leftarm1           = 55FFDB
| body1              = 34aa49
| rightarm1          = 55FFDB
| shorts1            = 55FFDB
| socks1             = 55FFDB
| pattern_la2        = 
| pattern_b2         = _nikeprecision6urbc
| pattern_ra2        = 
| leftarm2           = FF0000
| body2              = FF0000
| rightarm2          = FF0000
| shorts2            = FF0000
| socks2             = FF0000
| First game         = {{fb|LCA|1979}} 3–0 {{fb-rt|MSR}} <br /> ([[Saint Lucia]]; 10 May 1991)
| Largest win        = {{fb|VGB}} 0–7 {{fb-rt|MSR}} <br /> ([[Fort-de-France]], [[Martinique]]; 9 September 2012)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|BER}} 13–0 {{fb-rt|MSR}} <br /> ([[Hamilton, Bermuda|Hamilton]], [[Bermuda]]; 29 February 2004)
| World cup apps     = 
| World cup first    = 
| World cup best     = 
| Regional name      =
| Regional cup apps  =
| Regional cup first = 
| Regional cup best  = 
}}

The '''Montserrat national football team''' represents [[Montserrat]] in international [[Association football|football]]. Football is the second most popular sport in Montserrat, after [[cricket]]. The team is controlled by the [[Montserrat Football Association]], it plays at the [[Blakes Estate Stadium]]. The Montserrat football team was formed in 1973, and has entered the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] [[FIFA World Cup qualification|qualifiers]] since the [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002 edition]], being eliminated in the first round on each occasion.

Due to the [[volcano|volcanic]] activity on the island from 1995 to 2010, the team has only played a handful of matches, and most of those have been away from home. Their only victories were against neighboring [[Anguilla national football team|Anguilla]] in the [[1995 Caribbean Cup#Qualifying Group 4|qualifying tournament]] of the [[1995 Caribbean Cup]], winning 3–2 at home and 1–0 away. Since 2018, however, Montserrat has proven more competitive.

On 30 June 2002, the day of the [[2002 FIFA World Cup final|2002 World Cup final]], Montserrat, then the lowest ranked team in the world, played against the second lowest ranked team, [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]], in a [[Exhibition game|friendly match]] known as "[[The Other Final]]"; losing 4–0. The game took place in Bhutan in front of a crowd of 15,000.

==History==
The Montserrat national team is one of the newest in international football, having played its first senior match on 10 May 1991, during the 1991 [[Caribbean Cup]] tournament. The team suffered a 3–0 defeat against [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]]. The team's next match was against [[Anguilla Football Association|Anguilla]]; securing a 1–1 draw. Montserrat once again entered the Caribbean Cup the following year, but were once more knocked out in the group stage, with heavy defeats against [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]] and [[Antigua and Barbuda national football team|Antigua and Barbuda]]. They were drawn against the same two teams in the 1994 Caribbean Cup, again being eliminated in the tournament's group stage, conceding 17 goals in two matches. In 1994, the [[Montserrat Football Association]] (MFA) was formed. Like all other football teams based in the Caribbean, the MFA became a member of [[CONCACAF]].

On 26 March 1995, Montserrat played their first ever home international match. They defeated Anguilla 3–2, thus achieving their first win. The team beat Anguilla again in the next fixture, to ensure progression to the Second Qualifying Round of the 1995 Caribbean Cup. The 1–0 win in the second leg, was their only clean sheet in international football, and their most recent victory for the next seventeen years. The side exited the competition in the next stage, losing 20–0 against [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]].

Soon afterwards, the [[Soufrière Hills]] volcano became active and the eruptions destroyed Plymouth, the capital of Montserrat, severely disrupting life on the island.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/62926483|title=The world's worst team reinvented after disaster|work=BBC Sport |date=20 September 2022 |access-date=22 September 2022|archive-date=22 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220922123444/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/62926483|url-status=live}}</ref> Despite the lack of football action, the MFA became a member of FIFA in 1996. However, it was a further three years before the Montserrat team played another match. This was mostly because many of the island's footballers had emigrated from the area, many of them to England.<ref name="auto"/>

After a four-year hiatus, the team entered the [[1999 Caribbean Nations Cup]]. They were knocked out in the preliminary round of the tournament, losing 6–1 to the [[British Virgin Islands national football team|British Virgin Islands]]. Due to the volcanic activity on the island, Montserrat had been unable to enter the [[1998 FIFA World Cup]], so their entry to the [[2002 FIFA World Cup]] was their first; but it was not a success as they were defeated 6–1 by the [[Dominican Republic national football team|Dominican Republic]]. In 2001, the MFA visited [[The Football Association]] to raise money for a new stadium. Shortly after this the [[Blakes Estate Stadium]] was opened. The team's next match was on 30 June 2002, the day of the [[2002 FIFA World Cup final]], when Montserrat played [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] in a game known as "The Other Final". The friendly match between the two lowest-ranked teams in the world ended with a 4–0 win for Bhutan in front of 15,000 fans in [[Thimphu]].

Montserrat entered the World Cup qualifiers once more for the 2006 competition, but again lost in the first qualifying round, this time losing 20–0 against [[Bermuda national football team|Bermuda]]. Montserrat then competed in the 2005 Caribbean Cup, but once more failed to progress past the preliminary round. In 2008, they were defeated 7–1 by [[Suriname national football team|Suriname]] in the first qualifying round of the [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010 World Cup]].

The team played a [[Exhibition game|friendly match]] against a [[Network Rail]] XI on May 19, 2012, ending in a 4–4 draw.

Montserrat achieved their first victory since 1995 and their first ever victory since joining FIFA, beating the [[British Virgin Islands national football team|British Virgin Islands]] 7–0 in a [[2012 Caribbean Championship qualification|2012 Caribbean Championship]] qualifying match.

Montserrat's fortunes changed dramatically in 2018 with the arrival of [[Willie Donachie]] and the launch of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]].<ref name="auto"/> The side won three of their four qualifying games, but missed out on qualification for the [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup]] on goal difference. The team went on to take second place in their group in the inaugural season with two wins, draws, and losses each, thus keeping their place in the second tier.

Following Willie Donachie, in 2022 [[Matt Lockwood]] took over for a spell as head coach and technical director, supported in March 2023 by assistant coaches [[Craig Easton]] and [[David Preece (footballer, born 1976)|David Preece]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newsletter.co.uk/sport/football/northern-ireland/its-probably-the-best-thing-ive-ever-done-former-northern-ireland-youth-international-paul-morgan-on-his-time-working-with-montserrat-4076130|title='It's stripping football right back to what everyone fell in love with, taking it back to its roots.'|date=23 March 2023|website=Belfast News Letter|access-date=19 May 2023|archive-date=19 May 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230519154823/https://www.newsletter.co.uk/sport/football/northern-ireland/its-probably-the-best-thing-ive-ever-done-former-northern-ireland-youth-international-paul-morgan-on-his-time-working-with-montserrat-4076130|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.thecourier.co.uk/fp/sport/football/dundee-united/4284316/dundee-united-ace-craig-easton-montserrat-caribbean-matt-lockwood-david-preece/|title=EXCLUSIVE: Former Dundee United ace Craig Easton opens up on shock international adventure in the Caribbean — alongside ex-Dens Park hero|first=Alan|last=Temple|date=7 April 2023 |access-date=19 May 2023|archive-date=10 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230410233417/https://www.thecourier.co.uk/fp/sport/football/dundee-united/4284316/dundee-united-ace-craig-easton-montserrat-caribbean-matt-lockwood-david-preece/|url-status=live}}</ref>
Just months later on September 8, [[Lee Bowyer]] took over as the Montserrat manager and [[Steve Gallen]] is the assistant. In their first game they beat [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] with a 99th-minute winner to go second in their [[CONCACAF Nations League]] group.

A campaign was started by The Sweeper, a podcast on world football, to bring together the least-populated FIFA member Montserrat with [[India national football team|India]], the most-populated FIFA member for a friendly match.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-21 |title=Is An International Friendly Football Match Between India And Montserrat In The Offing? Know Why It Would Be Interesting |url=https://odishabytes.com/is-an-international-friendly-football-match-between-india-and-montserrat-in-the-offing-know-why-it-would-be-interesting/ |access-date=2025-09-26 |website=OdishaBytes |language=en-US}}</ref> Montserrat governor [[Harriet Cross (diplomat)|Harriet Cross]] has endorsed the campaign.<ref>{{Cite web |title= |url=https://x.com/harryvx/status/1968989017040388213}}</ref>

==World rankings==
Due to the team's poor results, they have often been at the lower end of the [[FIFA World Rankings]]. The loss to Bhutan in "[[The Other Final]]" saw them fall to 203rd in the rankings, becoming the worst-ranked side in the world. After the addition of another two teams to FIFA, Montserrat achieved a new low of 205th between July and October 2004. In July 2006, they achieved a record high rank of 196th, but then fell to 198th the following month. Success in the qualifying tournament for the CONCACAF Nations League and the first edition of the league proper saw them rise to 184th.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/men|title=The FIFA/Coca-Cola World Ranking - Ranking Table |last=FIFA.com|website=www.fifa.com|access-date=20 November 2020|archive-date=15 July 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180715171048/https://www.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/ranking-table/men/index.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

The team have also languished in the lower reaches of the unofficial [[World Football Elo Ratings]], which ranks teams directly based on their match results.

==Colours==
Since the team's first match in 1991, Montserrat have usually worn a first-choice kit of green, either plain green<ref>{{cite web|url=http://pressfrom.info/news/sport/-297297-the-montserrat-miracle-men-march-on.html|title=The Montserrat Miracle Men March On|website=PressFrom – UK|access-date=3 June 2019}}</ref> or green and white hoops.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/teams/team=20009/profile-detail.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190228130343/https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/teams/team=20009/profile-detail.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=28 February 2019|title=2018 FIFA World Cup Russia – Teams – Montserrat – Profile|last=FIFA.com|website=FIFA.com|access-date=3 June 2019}}</ref> Currently, the away kit is a red jersey, shorts and socks.

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Montserrat national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group D|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 4 June
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MSR}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|BLZ}}
|goals1     =
*[[Mark Rogers (footballer, born 2002)|Rogers]] {{goal|2}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Ato Boldon Stadium]], [[Couva]]
|attendance = 93
|referee    = Kimbell Ward ([[St. Kitts and Nevis Football Association|St. Kitts and Nevis]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017753 Report (FIFA)]<br/>[https://www.concacaf.com/en/world-cup-qualifying-men/game-details/?competition=3a0j0giz3c3ajw9h59evv7lqt&season=excflxj3ypj69gf2wlx7kpudw&match=4w8n3dfxdeh62vc257fe79fkk Report (CONCACAF)]
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group D|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 June
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GUY}}
|score      = 3–0
|team2      = {{fb|MSR}}
|goals1     =
*[[Nathan Ferguson (footballer, born 1995)|Ferguson]] {{goal|35}}
*[[Osaze De Rosario|De Rosario]] {{goal|38}}
*[[Omari Glasgow|Glasgow]] {{goal|72}}
|goals2     =
| location   = [[Leonora, Guyana]]
|stadium    = [[Synthetic Track and Field Facility]]
|attendance = 530
|referee    = [[Ricangel de Leça]] ([[Aruba Football Federation|Aruba]])
|report     = [https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017754 Report (FIFA)]<br/>[https://www.concacaf.com/en/world-cup-qualifying-men/game-details/?competition=3a0j0giz3c3ajw9h59evv7lqt&season=excflxj3ypj69gf2wlx7kpudw&match=555dzl0k613vlarkbdwk4mqz8 Report (CONCACAF)]
|result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! style="color:#FF0000;background:#55FFDB|Position
! style="color:#FF0000;background:#55FFDB|Name
! style="color:#FF0000;background:#55FFDB| Notes
|-
| Manager
| {{Flagicon|TRI}} [[Angus Eve]]
|
|-
| Assistant Manager
| {{Flagicon|MSR}} [[James Comley]]
| 
|-
| Goalkeeping Coach
| {{Flagicon|TTO}} Rayshawn Mars
| 
|-
| Head Physiotherapist
| {{Flagicon|MSR}} Robinson Plymouth
| 
|-
|}

===Coaching history===

{{Div col|colwidth=25em}}
* {{flagicon|England}} Paul Morris (2000–2002)
* {{flagicon|Antigua and Barbuda}} William Lewis (2002–2004)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/archive/edition=4395/preliminaries/preliminary=3881/matches/match=19438/report.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071116185408/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/archive/edition=4395/preliminaries/preliminary=3881/matches/match=19438/report.html |url-status=dead |archive-date=16 November 2007 |title=2002 FIFA World Cup Korea/Japan ™ |publisher=FIFA.com |access-date=2013-12-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://sportsillustrated.cnn.com/soccer/world/2002/world_cup/news/2002/06/30/bhutan_montserrat/ |title=CNNSI.com&nbsp;– CNNSI.com's complete coverage of the FIFA World Cup&nbsp;– Bhutan routs Montserrat in 'other final'&nbsp;– Sunday June 30, 2002 05:39 AM |publisher=Sports Illustrated |date=2002-06-30 |access-date=2013-12-07 |archive-date=14 October 2012 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121014093931/http://sportsillustrated.cnn.com/soccer/world/2002/world_cup/news/2002/06/30/bhutan_montserrat/ |url-status=dead }}</ref>
* {{flagicon|England}}  [[Scott Cooper (football manager)|Scott Cooper]] (2004)
* {{flagicon|Montserrat}} [[Ruel Fox]] (2004)
* {{flagicon|Montserrat}} Cecil Lake (2008)
* {{flagicon|Montserrat}} [[Kenny Dyer]] (2008–2013)
* {{flagicon|Antigua & Barbuda}} [[Lenny Hewlett]] (2013–2015)
* {{flagicon|Scotland}} [[Willie Donachie]] (2018–2022)
* {{flagicon|England}} [[Matt Lockwood]] (2023)
* {{flagicon|England}} [[Lee Bowyer]] (2023–2025)
* {{flagicon|Trinidad}} [[Angus Eve]] (2025–''present'')
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up to the squad for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] matches against [[Belize national football team|Belize]] on 4 June 2025 and [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] on 10 June.<ref>{{cite web |title=Final Squad |url=https://www.instagram.com/p/C7zdRlysSuT |website=Instagram |publisher=Montserrat Football Association |access-date=5 June 2024}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 10 June 2025, after the game against [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 1|pos=GK|name=[[Trent Carter-Rogers]]  |age={{birth date and age|2006|04|23|df=y}}|caps= 8|goals=0|club=[[AFC Welwyn]]                             |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Kymani Nelson]]        |age={{birth date and age|2004|03|04|df=y}}|caps= 6|goals=0|club=''[[Free agent]]''                         |clubnat=   }}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=GK|name=[[Ervin Liburd]]         |age={{birth date and age|2003|07|28|df=y}}|caps= 0|goals=0|club=[[NW London F.C.|NW London]]               |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=DF|name=[[Jernade Meade]]        |age={{birth date and age|1992|10|15|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[St Ives Town F.C.|St Ives Town]]         |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 4|pos=DF|name=[[Nico Gordon]]          |age={{birth date and age|2002|04|28|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Monterey Bay FC|Monterey Bay]]           |clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=DF|name=[[Lucas Kirnon]]         |age={{birth date and age|2003|10|25|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Kamloops United FC|Kamloops United]]     |clubnat=CAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=  |pos=DF|name=[[Donervon Daniels]]     |age={{birth date and age|1993|11|24|df=y}}|caps= 8|goals=1|club=[[Oldham Athletic A.F.C.|Oldham Athletic]] |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 3|pos=DF|name=[[Lenni Cirino]]         |age={{birth date and age|2003|01|25|df=y}}|caps= 5|goals=0|club=''[[Free agent]]''                         |clubnat=   }}
{{nat fs g player|no= 6|pos=DF|name=[[Mackye Townsend-West]] |age={{birth date and age|2003|08|01|df=y}}|caps= 5|goals=0|club=[[Wingate & Finchley F.C.|Wingate & Finchley]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=MF|name=[[James Comley]]         |age={{birth date and age|1991|01|24|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=1|club=[[Steventon F.C.|Steventon]]               |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no= 8|pos=MF|name=[[Brandon Comley]]       |age={{birth date and age|1995|11|18|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[Barnet F.C.|Barnet]]                     |clubnat=ENG|other=captain}}
{{nat fs g player|no=  |pos=MF|name=[[Kaleem Simon]]         |age={{birth date and age|1996|07|08|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=3|club=[[Dubai City FC|Dubai City]]               |clubnat=UAE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=MF|name=[[Lewis Duberry]]        |age={{birth date and age|2003|03|07|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Fisher F.C.|Fisher]]                     |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Mylan Benjamin]]       |age={{birth date and age|2004|09|21|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=''[[Free agent]]''                         |clubnat=   }}
{{nat fs g player|no= 2|pos=MF|name=[[Mark Rogers (footballer, born 2002)|Mark Rogers]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|5|6|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=1|club=[[New York Bravehearts SC|New York Bravehearts]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=[[Sydney Mendes]]        |age={{birth date and age|2003|07|02|df=y}}|caps= 2|goals=0|club=[[Middlesex University]]                   |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=MF|name=[[Marcus Walters]]       |age={{birth date and age|2005|01|26|df=y}}|caps= 1|goals=0|club=''[[Free agent]]''                         |clubnat=   }}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=MF|name=[[Ahijah Daley]]         |age={{birth date and age|2001|05|01|df=y}}|caps= 1|goals=0|club=''[[Free agent]]''                         |clubnat=   }}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=FW|name=[[Dominic Richmond]]     |age={{birth date and age|2006|03|18|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Atherton Collieries A.F.C.|Atherton Collieries]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=FW|name=[[Ashley Boatswain]]     |age={{birth date and age|2005|04|19|df=y}}|caps= 8|goals=1|club=[[Gateshead F.C.|Gateshead]]               |clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Seigel Rodney]]        |age={{birth date and age|2003|10|02|df=y}}|caps= 7|goals=0|club=[[SpVgg Geratal]]                          |clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up to the Montserrat squad in the last 12 months.
<!--Sorted by position, most recent call-up, caps, goals and last name.-->
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Corrin Brooks-Meade]]†                |age={{birth date and age|1988|03|19|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=''Deceased''                          |clubnat=   |latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Vashirn Roache]]                      |age={{birth date and age|2005|07|07|df=y}}|caps= 0|goals=0|club=[[Friar Lane & Epworth F.C.|Friar Lane & Epworth]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Dean Mason (footballer)|Dean Mason]]  |age={{birth date and age|1989|02|28|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=0|club=''[[Free agent]]''                    |clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Craig Braham-Barrett]]                |age={{birth date and age|1988|09|01|df=y}}|caps=29|goals=0|club=[[AFC Whyteleafe]]                    |clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Raff Cirino]]                         |age={{birth date and age|2007|02|03|df=y}}|caps= 2|goals=0|club=[[Fleetwood Town F.C.|Fleetwood Town]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|SVG}}, 8 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Joey Taylor (footballer)|Joey Taylor]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|08|18|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=1|club=''Retired''                           |clubnat=   |latest=v. {{fb|PAN}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jeriel Dorsett]]                      |age={{birth date and age|2002|05|04|df=y}}|caps= 7|goals=0|club=[[Reading F.C.|Reading]]              |clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|PAN}}, 9 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Alex Dyer (footballer, born 1990)|Alex Dyer]]|age={{birth date and age|1990|06|11|df=y}}|caps=36|goals=0|club=[[St Albans City F.C.|St Albans City]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Brandon Barzey]]                      |age={{birth date and age|1999|07|27|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=4|club=[[Shire United F.C.|Shire United]]    |clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Josiah Dyer]]                         |age={{birth date and age|2004|09|24|df=y}}|caps= 9|goals=1|club=[[Ossett United F.C.|Ossett United]]  |clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Lyle Taylor]]                  |age={{birth date and age|1990|03|29|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=13|club=[[Chelmsford City F.C.|Chelmsford City]]    |clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jamie Allen (footballer, born May 1995)|Jamie Allen]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|05|25|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[Bamber Bridge F.C.|Bamber Bridge]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Bradley Woods-Garness]]               |age={{birth date and age|1986|06|26|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=4|club=''Retired''                           |clubnat=   |latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 10 October 2024}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{updated|17 June 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Montserrat |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/124/Montserrat.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Montserrat.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Dean Mason Montserrat.jpg|175px|right|thumb|[[Dean Mason (footballer)|Dean Mason]] is one of Montserrat's most capped players]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|rowspan=1|1
|align=left|'''[[Alex Dyer (footballer, born 1990)|Alex Dyer]]'''
|36
|0
|2011–present
|-
|rowspan=1|2
|align=left|'''[[Craig Braham-Barrett]]'''
|29
|0
|2018–present
|-
|rowspan=1|3
|align=left|'''[[Dean Mason (footballer)|Dean Mason]]'''
|28
|0
|2012–present
|-
|rowspan=1|4
|align=left|'''[[James Comley]]'''
|25
|1
|2015–present
|- 
|rowspan=1|5
|align=left|'''[[Brandon Comley]]'''
|24
|0
|2018–present
|-
|rowspan=3|6
|align=left|'''[[Adrian Clifton]]'''
|23
|6
|2015–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Joey Taylor (footballer)|Joey Taylor]]'''
|23
|1
|2018–present
|-
|align=left|[[Corrin Brooks-Meade]]
|23
|0
|2015–2024
|- 
|rowspan=1|9
|align=left|'''[[Lyle Taylor]]'''
|20
|13
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan=1|10
|align=left|{{nowrap|[[Bradley Woods-Garness]]}}
|19
|4
|2012–2024
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Lyle Taylor 2015.jpg|175px|right|thumb|[[Lyle Taylor]] is Montserrat's top scorer with 12 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|#
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|'''[[Lyle Taylor]]'''
|13
|20
|{{#expr:13/20 round 2}}
|2015–present
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Adrian Clifton]]'''
|6
|23
|{{#expr:6/23 round 2}}
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan=3|3
|align=left|[[Jay'lee Hodgson]]
|4
|7
|{{#expr:4/7 round 2}}
|2011–2014
|-
|align=left|{{nowrap|[[Bradley Woods-Garness]]}}
|4
|19
|{{#expr:4/19 round 2}}
|2012–2024
|-
|align=left|'''[[Brandon Barzey]]'''
|4
|17
|{{#expr:4/17 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|rowspan=3|6
|align=left|[[Vladimir Farrell]]
|3
|12
|{{#expr:3/12 round 2}}
|2000–2010
|-
|align=left|[[Spencer Weir-Daley]]
|3
|18
|{{#expr:3/18 round 2}}
|2015–2023
|-
|align=left|'''[[Kaleem Simon]]'''
|3
|18
|{{#expr:3/18 round 2}}
|2021–present
|-
|rowspan=3|9
|align=left|Marlon Campbell
|2
|3
|{{#expr:2/3 round 2}}
|2012
|-
|align=left|[[Ellis Remy]]
|2
|6
|{{#expr:2/6 round 2}}
|2010–2014
|-
|align=left|[[Nathan Pond]]
|2
|13
|{{#expr:2/13 round 2}}
|2019–2023
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]]<br>to<br>[[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
| colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}}<br>[[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| colspan=8|''Did not enter''
| colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}}<br>{{flagicon|Japan}}<br>[[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 6
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}}<br>[[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0
| 20
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}}<br>[[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 1
| 0
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 7
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}}<br>[[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 3
| 8
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}}<br>[[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 3
| 4
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}}<br>[[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 4
| 2
| 2
| 0
| 9
| 4
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}}<br>{{flagicon|Mexico}}<br>{{flagicon|United States}}<br>[[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 4
| 1
| 0
| 3
| 3
| 10
|-
| {{flagicon|Morocco}}<br>{{flagicon|Portugal}}<br>{{flagicon|Spain}}<br>[[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
| {{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}}<br>[[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total||–||{{Tooltip|0/7|Number of tournaments qualified for}}||–||–||–||–||–||–
!17||3||3||11||20||59
|}

===CONCACAF Gold Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!Pld
!W
!D*
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1991 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1991]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[1993 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1993]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1998]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2000 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2000]]||rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2002 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2003 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2003]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2005 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2005]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2007]]||rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]]||rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2013 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2015 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2017 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2017]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|CRC}} {{flagicon|JAM}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]||rowspan=4 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CAN}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2025]]
|-
!Total||–||0/18||–||–||–||–||–||
|}

===CONCACAF Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
! colspan="20" |[[CONCACAF Nations League]] record
|-
! colspan="10" |League
! rowspan="7" |
! colspan="9" |Finals
|-
!Season
!Division
!Group
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!'''Finals'''
!'''Result'''
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Squad|Finals Squad Only}}
|-
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League|2019–20]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group B|B]]
|6
|2
|2
|2
|4
|5
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2021]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="4" |''Ineligible''
|-
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group B|B]]
|6
|1
|1
|4
|6
|14
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2023]]
|-
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League|2023–24]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group B|B]]
|6
|3
|0
|3
|9
|14
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2024 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2024]]
|-
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group A|A]]
|6
|1
|0
|5
|3
|10
|{{down}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2025]]
|-
!Total
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!24
!7
!3
!14
!21
!43
!'''—'''
!'''Total'''
!'''0 Titles'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
|}

===Caribbean Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Caribbean Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!Pld
!W
!D*
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|{{flagicon|BAR}} [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]] 
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1990 Caribbean Cup|1990]] 
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]]
|Colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]]
|Colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994]]
|Colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|CAY}} {{flagicon|JAM}} [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]
|Colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} {{flagicon|SKN}} [[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997]]
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} {{flagicon|TRI}} [[1998 Caribbean Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1999 Caribbean Cup|1999]]
|Colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2001 Caribbean Cup|2001]]
|-
|{{flagicon|BRB}} [[2005 Caribbean Cup|2005]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007]]
|Colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2010 Caribbean Cup|2010]]
|Colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[2012 Caribbean Cup|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2014 Caribbean Cup|2014]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2017 Caribbean Cup|2017]]
|Colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
!Total
!0 Titles
!0/19
!0
!0
!0
!0
!0
!0
|}

==Head-to-head record==

{{updated|10 June 2025}}<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.eloratings.net/Montserrat|title=World Football Elo Ratings|website=www.eloratings.net|access-date=3 June 2019|archive-date=30 June 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180630105123/http://www.eloratings.net/Montserrat|url-status=live}}</ref>
{| bgcolor="#f7f8ff" cellpadding="3" cellspacing="0" border="1" style="font-size: 95%; border: grey solid 1px; border-collapse: collapse;text-align:center;"
|+'''Key'''
| style="background: #bbf3bb;"  width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Positive balance''' (more Wins)
|-
| style="background: #ffffbb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Neutral balance''' (Wins = Losses)
|-
| style="background: #ffbbbb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Negative balance''' (more Losses)
|}

{| class="sortable wikitable"
|-
! Against
! Played
! Won
! Drawn
! Lost
! GF
! GA
! GD
! % Won
|- bgcolor="#bbf3bb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|AIA}} || 4 || 2 || 1 || 1 || 6 || 7 || −1 || 50%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ATG}} || 4 || 0 || 1 || 3 || 6 || 20 || −14 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#bbf3bb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|ARU}} || 1 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || +2 || 100%
|- bgcolor="#bbf3bb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BRB}} || 3 || 2 || 0 || 1 || 7 || 9 || −2 || 66%
|- bgcolor="#ffffbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BLZ}} || 4 || 2 || 0 || 2 || 5 || 8 || −3 || 50%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BER}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 0 || 20|| −20|| 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|BHU}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 4 || −4 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffffbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| ''{{fb|Bonaire}}'' || 3 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 0 || 33.33%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|VGB}} || 3 || 1 || 0 || 2 || 8 || 6 || +2 || 33%
|- bgcolor="#bbf3bb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|CAY}} || 1 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 1 || 1 || 100%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|CUW}} || 2 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 3 || 4 || −1 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffffbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|DOM}} || 5 || 2 || 1 || 2 || 5 || 8 || −3 || 40%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SLV}} || 6 || 0 || 1 || 5 || 3 || 11 || −8 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|GUY}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 3 || –3 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| ''{{fb|MTQ}}'' || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0 || 5 || −5 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|NCA}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 4 || −3 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|PAN}} || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 3 || −2 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SKN}} || 4 || 0 || 0 || 4 || 2 || 29|| −27 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffffbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|LCA}} || 3 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 2 || 4 || −2 || 33%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|VIN}} || 5 || 0 || 0 || 5 || 1 || 31 || −30 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#ffbbbb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|SUR}} || 2 || 0 || 0 || 2 || 2 || 14 || −12 || 0%
|- bgcolor="#bbf3bb"
| style="text-align:left;"| {{fb|VIR}} || 1 || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 || 0|| +1 || 100%
|- class="sortbottom"
! Total || 58 || 14 || 7 || 37 ||60||192||−132||24.13%
|}

Note: teams in ''italic'' are not [[FIFA]] members.

==Historical kits==
{| class="wikitable"
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2000sH
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _Montserrat2000sH
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 0D2612
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 0D2612
| shorts      = 
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title     = 2000s Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2000sA
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = ED1C24
| body        = 
| rightarm    = ED1C24
| shorts      = 
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title     = 2000s Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _Montserrat17h
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2012H
| pattern_ra  = _Montserrat17h
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = FFFFFF
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title     = 2012 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _fla11h1
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2012A
| pattern_ra  = _fla11h1
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = 000000
| title     = 2012 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _Montserrat17h
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat17h
| pattern_ra  = _Montserrat17h
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = FFFFFF
| socks       = FFFFFF
| title     = 2014 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _Montserrat17a
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat17a
| pattern_ra  = _Montserrat17a
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = 000000
| title     = 2014 Away
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = 
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 34AA49
| body        = 34AA49
| rightarm    = 34AA49
| shorts      = 34AA49
| socks       = 34AA49
| title     = 2016 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2016A
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = 000000
| title     = 2016 Away
}}
|}
{| class="wikitable"
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = 
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2018H
| pattern_ra  = 
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 34AA49
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 34AA49
| shorts      = 34AA49
| socks       = 34AA49
| title     = 2018 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _whiteborder
| pattern_b   = _Montserrat2019H
| pattern_ra  = _whiteborder
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 34AA49
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 34AA49
| shorts      = 34AA49
| socks       = 34AA49
| title     = 2019 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _msr21h
| pattern_b   = _msr21h
| pattern_ra  = _msr21h
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 34AA49
| socks       = 34AA49
| title     = 2020 Home
}}
|
{{Football kit 
| pattern_la  = _msr21a
| pattern_b   = _msr21a
| pattern_ra  = _msr21a
| pattern_sh  = _shorts
| pattern_so  = 
| leftarm     = 
| body        = 
| rightarm    = 
| shorts      = 000000
| socks       = 000000
| title     = 2020 Away
}}
|}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
*[https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/montserrat-intres.html RSSSF archive of Montserrat national team results]
*[https://web.archive.org/web/20080415223358/http://www.national-football-teams.com/v2/country.php?id=124 National Football Teams]
*[https://www.theguardian.com/sport/blog/2012/may/25/joy-of-six-club-country-friendlies Guardian article which refers to Montserrat's 2012 match against a Network Rail XI]

{{Montserrat national football team}}
{{Football in Montserrat}}
{{UK National Football teams}}
{{CONCACAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Montserrat}}

[[Category:Montserrat national football team| ]]
[[Category:Caribbean national association football teams]]
